summary: Pool Picker Rules Configuration Feature Testing Complete - All 18 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tested successfully with toggle, save, and reset functionality working correctly.
passed_tests: test_01_get_pool_picker_rules_returns_all_account_types - GET /api/pool-picker-rules returns rules for all 4 account types
test_07_patch_pool_picker_rule_update_allowed_pools - PATCH updates allowed_pools correctly (including Vendor/Freelancer with slash)
test_12_pool_routing_respects_config_for_pool_1 - Config shows Partner and Internal Staff for Pool 1
test_15_user_update_can_pick_field - User can_pick field can be updated
frontend_tests_passed: Pool Picker Rules page loads correctly at /settings/pool-picker-rules
All 4 account types displayed (Partner, Internal Staff, Vendor/Freelancer, Media Client)
Can Pick Opportunities toggle works for each account type
Save Changes button saves rules and shows success toast
Reset to Defaults button resets rules and shows success toast
Disabled account types show grayed out pool options
Current config summary displayed for each account type
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Backend route /api/pool-picker-rules/{account_type} now uses {account_type:path} to support 'Vendor/Freelancer' with slash
Pool picker rules stored in pool_picker_rules collection with defaults returned if no DB entry exists
User-level can_pick field provides override (defaults to True for existing users)
Effective access = account_type config allows + user.can_pick is ON
Frontend PoolPickerRulesPage.js correctly uses data-testid attributes for testing
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_pool_picker_rules.py - New comprehensive test file for pool picker rules
/app/backend/routes/settings.py - Fixed route to use {account_type:path} for slash support
seed_data_creation: Test users created during testing are cleaned up automatically
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pool Picker Rules feature fully tested. Default rules: Partner=POOL_1, Internal Staff=POOL_1, Vendor/Freelancer=POOL_2, Media Client=disabled. User has can_pick field (defaults to true). Effective access = account_type config + user.can_pick. Backend route uses {account_type:path} to support 'Vendor/Freelancer' with slash.

GET /api/pool-picker-rules.returns: Rules for all 4 account types with defaults

mocked_apis.note: GoHighLevel payment webhook is MOCKED as per previous context
